articles.title: Trump to address nation on Iran war. And, SCOTUS considers birthright citizenship
articles.summary: Trump is set to address the nation after saying the U.S. will withdraw from Iran in 2-3 weeks. And, the president will be at the Supreme Court today as arguments on birthright citizenship begin.

articles.title: Israel’s Message to Southern Lebanon: Shiites Must Go
articles.summary: Israel has issued sweeping evacuation warnings, and pressed some Christian and Druse leaders to expel Shiite Muslims from their towns, the leaders said.

articles.title: Trump to Address the Nation About War Plans, and TMZ Goes After Congress
articles.summary: Plus, what to know about NASA’s moon mission.

articles.title: How Immigration Policies Like Birthright Citizenship Shaped the Supreme Court Justices’ Histories
articles.summary: As the justices prepare to hear a landmark case about birthright citizenship, their family stories are a reminder that the law has shaped who can be an American.

articles.title: Supreme Court to Hear Landmark Challenge to Birthright Citizenship
articles.summary: The justices will consider the constitutionality of President Trump’s executive order ending birthright citizenship for the children of undocumented people and some temporary foreign visitors.

articles.title: He Won Birthright Citizenship for All. His Own Family Never Knew.
articles.summary: Wong Kim Ark brought his case to the Supreme Court in 1898. But some of his descendants didn’t even know his name until about 15 years ago.

articles.title: From scrappy startup to tech giant, Apple celebrates its 50th year
articles.summary: Countercultural mythmaking and global corporate dominance have helped the tech corporation sail through criticism.

articles.title: 4 takeaways from the U.S. men's national soccer team's World Cup tune-up games
articles.summary: The two losses — 5-2 to Belgium and 2-0 to Portugal — were a wakeup call for the USMNT and the team's ambitions for this summer's World Cup. But the Americans said they welcomed the lessons learned.

articles.title: Homesick in a foreign country, a teenager meets a lifelong friend
articles.summary: Holly Deiaco-Smith was feeling homesick while studying abroad in France when she was 19 years old. An encounter at the post office changed everything and led to a decades-long friendship.

articles.title: Supreme Court considers a historic case about who is — and isn't — born a citizen
articles.summary: At issue is President Trump's challenge to a constitutional provision that has long been interpreted to guarantee American citizenship to every child born in the United States.

articles.title: NASA's Artemis II astronauts are hours away from moon launch. Watch it here
articles.summary: NASA's Artemis II mission aims to send four astronauts around the moon on a roughly 10-day journey that could help pave the way toward a future lunar landing.

articles.title: Morning news brief
articles.summary: Trump tells allies who need Strait of Hormuz for oil to get it themselves, how the Iran war is impacting the U.S. and global economy, SCOTUS to hear arguments on birthright citizenship.

articles.title: After years in Russian captivity, former mayor of Ukrainian city returns home
articles.summary: After spending most of the full-scale war in Russian captivity, the former mayor of a frontline Ukrainian city recalls his ordeal as he returns to a hometown deeply transformed by modern warfare.

articles.title: Food assistance slashed for hundreds of thousands of Rohingya refugees trapped in Bangladesh camps
articles.summary: Hundreds of thousands of Rohingya refugees struggling to survive in Bangladesh's overcrowded camps will see their food assistance slashed starting on Wednesday, raising alarm throughout the increasing

articles.title: Bomb Shelters and a Drone-Proof Roof: Trump Says Ballroom Is a Matter of Security
articles.summary: President Trump spoke about his ballroom’s security features as he argued against a judge’s orders to stop construction.

articles.title: Trump Signs Order Seeking Federal Control of Mail Voting as He Promotes False Claims
articles.summary: Election experts and Democratic officials called the order legally invalid, and Arizona and Oregon pledged to immediately challenge it in court.

articles.title: Kherson, Ukraine: A hometown changed (Pt. 1)
articles.summary: Four years of Russia's all-out war on Ukraine have transformed not only Ukrainian cities but also how modern warfare is waged, in the first of this two-part story from Kherson.

articles.title: Iranians debate whether the war is worth it
articles.summary: As the war in Iran enters its second month, and President Trump signals an end to the war, many Iranians are urging the U.S and Israel to keep striking their country.

articles.title: One of the first people known to change their gender was an ancient Egyptian pharaoh
articles.summary: NPR art director and illustrator Jackie Lay tells the story of Hatshepsut, who is widely regarded as one of the greatest pharaohs in Egypt's history — but whose legacy was erased for over 3,000 years.

articles.title: 6 books named finalists for the 2026 International Booker Prize
articles.summary: The shortlisted titles include novels and novellas from authors and translators spanning four continents, with stories that range from Japanese-controlled 1930s Taiwan to the streets of Tehran in 1979

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
